zh.lifeSummary: Beatrix De Monte Alto於1268年出生在英國英格蘭。她和William DeMalpas至少有1個兒子。她去世於1322年在她的家鄉享年54歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Beatrix De Monte Alto于1268年出生在英国英格兰。她和William DeMalpas至少有1个儿子。她去世于1322年在她的家乡享年54岁。

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Monte
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Carlo, Mario, Domenic, Salvatore, Aldo, Angelo, Antonio, Arturo, Carmelo, Gaspare, Carmela, Ciro, Gasper, Philomena.  Spanish Jose, Manuel, Carlos, Edgardo, Emilia.
Italian (southern), Spanish, and Portuguese: topographic name for someone who lived on or near a mountain, Italian, Spanish, Portuguese monte (from Latin mons, genitive montis).
Italian and Jewish (from Italy): habitational name from any of the places called with Monte or Monti. Compare   Del Monte  .

variants.name: Monti
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Salvatore, Aniello, Pasquale, Aldo, Carmel, Elio, Enrico, Gaetano, Marco, Marino, Philomena.
Italian: variant of   Monte  , from the plural.

variants.name: Montagna
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Sal, Gaetano, Alessandro, Antonino, Attilio, Carlo, Carmelo, Cosmo, Dino, Enrico, Oreste.

Italian: topographic name for someone who lived on or near a mountain or mountains, Italian montagna, from Late Latin montanea, originally the neuter plural of an adjective derivative of mons (see Monte 1), or a habitational name from one of the many places called with this word.

variants.name: Montini
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Enio, Pietro, Arsenio, Concetta, Dante, Dino, Domenic, Eliseo, Lorenzo, Marco, Mauro, Quirino, Ricco, Tulio.
Italian (Milan):
patronymic or plural form of a diminutive of   Monte  .

variants.name: Montes
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Jesus, Manuel, Jorge, Luis, Francisco, Miguel, Carlos, Alfredo, Ramon, Raul.  Portuguese Joaquim.

Spanish (also Montés); Portuguese (also Montês): topographic name for a mountain dweller, from the plural form of Monte ‘mountain’ or from the adjective Spanish montés, Portuguese montês ‘of the mountain’.

variants.name: Montello
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Biagio, Carmela, Marco.
Italian: from a diminutive of   Monte  .

variants.name: Del Monte
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Gaspar, Angelo, Rocco, Giovanni, Pompeo, Antonio, Armelio, Berta, Camillo, Carmela, Deno, Elvio, Ezio, Gilda, Giulio.  Spanish Angel, Gonzalo, Guillermina, Jesus, Jose, Jose Manuel.
Italian, Spanish, and Jewish (from Italy): variant, with the preposition and definite article del ‘from the’, of Monte .

Jewish (Sephardic): adoption of the Spanish surname (see 1 above) at the moment of conversion to Roman Catholicism. After the return to Judaism (generations later), some descendants retained the name their families used as Catholics.

variants.name: Montejo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Raul, Carlos, Eduardo, Abelardo, Alberto, Julio, Mario, Mauricio, Miguel, Miguel Angel, Alejandro, Alfonso.
Spanish:
topographic name from a diminutive of   Monte   1.

variants.name: Montesi
meanings: Italian:
habitational name for someone from a place called   Monte  , from an adjectival form of the placename.
from the plural of Montese, a habitational name from Montese in Modena province.

variants.name: Montas
meanings: Spanish and Catalan (Montás); French: derivative of Spanish monte, Catalan and Old French mont ‘mountain’ (see Monte , Mont ). This surname is most common in the Dominican Republic and Haiti.

nameMeanings.name: Alto
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Finnish Arvo, Reino, Eino, Sulo, Waino.
Altered form of Finnish   Aalto  .
Italian, Spanish, and Portuguese: nickname for a big man, from alto ‘tall, big’.

variants.name: Aalto
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Finnish Tauno, Ensio, Pentti, Reino, Sulo, Tarmo, Wilho.  Scandinavian Hilma, Johan.
Finnish: ornamental name from aalto ‘wave’.

nameMeanings.name: Beatrix
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From a Late Latin personal name, which was borne by a saint martyred in Rome, together with Saints Faustinus and Simplicius, in the early 4th century. The original form of the name was probably Viatrix, a feminine version of Viator ‘voyager (through life)’, which was a favourite name among early Christians. This was then altered by association with Latin Beatus ‘blessed’ (Via- and Bea- sometimes being pronounced the same in Late Latin). See also Beatrice .

en.lifeSummary: Beatrix De Monte Alto was born in 1268, in England, United Kingdom. She had at least 1 son with William DeMalpas. She died in 1322, in her hometown, at the age of 54.

fr.lifeSummary: Beatrix De Monte Alto est née en 1268, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Elle a eu au moins 1 fils avec William DeMalpas. Elle est décédée en 1322, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 54 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Beatrix De Monte Alto wurde 1268 in England, United Kingdom geboren. Sie hatte mit William DeMalpas mindestens 1 Sohn. Sie starb 1322 im Alter von 54 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Beatrix De Monte Alto è nata il nel 1268 nel Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Lei avuto almeno 1 figlio con William DeMalpas. Morì nel 1322, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 54 anni.

ja.Malpas, Cheshire, England: イギリスチェシュアイアマルプアス
ja.lifeSummary: Beatrix De Monte Altoは1268年にイギリスイギリスで生まれた。彼女はWilliam DeMalpasとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼女は1322年に故郷で54歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Beatrix De Monte Alto(은)는 1268년 영국 잉글랜드에서 태어났다. 그녀는 William DeMalpas(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1322년 그녀의 고향에서 54세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Beatrix De Monte Alto nasceu em 1268, em Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Ela teve pelo menos 1 filho com William DeMalpas. Ela faleceu em 1322, em sua cidade natal, com 54 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Beatrix De Monte Alto родилась в 1268 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания. У неё был по крайней мере 1 сын от William DeMalpas. Она умерла в 1322 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 54 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Beatrix De Monte Alto nació en 1268, en Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con William DeMalpas. Murió en 1322, en su pueblo, a la edad de 54 años.

th.Malpas, Cheshire, England: แมลปาส, ชส์ฮิเร, เอิงแลนด์
